results.extraction_notes: Agents explicitly finalized the split in turns 14-16; turn 15 contains confirmation by mturk_agent_1 accepting the split.
Both agents explicitly confirmed the deal (Turn 12 and Turn 13): Agent 1 gets 3 Food and 2 Firewood; Agent 2 gets 1 Firewood and 3 Water.

Although both agents verbally agreed to a split in Turn 16, the stated allocations (agent_1: 3 Water,1 Food,1 Firewood; agent_2: 2 Water,2 Food,2 Firewood) are inconsistent with the known totals (only 3 Water total), so no clear valid deal can be inferred.
No explicit acceptance of the final proposed allocation; last turn is a proposal but not confirmed.
Agents explicitly agreed to these allocations (agent_1 confirmed the deal in Turn 13 after agent_2 proposed it in Turn 12).
Both agents explicitly confirm the same split in turns 7-9; agent_1 finally confirms agreement in turn 9.
Both agents explicitly confirm the same allocation in turns 11-12, with agent 2's turn 12 providing the final confirmation.
Turn 10 contains an explicit final split: agent_2 gets 2 Firewood,1 Food,1 Water; agent_1 gets the remaining packages.
No explicit final acceptance reached; conversation ends with repeated proposals but no clear agreement.
No single final agreed allocation: turn 10 accepted an earlier proposal but subsequent turns continue negotiating different splits with no mutual acceptance.

Turn 6 contains the agreed terms: agent1 gets all 3 water, firewood split, and food split 2-1; agent2 previously sought more food so assigned 2 food and 2 firewood (remaining). Confirmation at turn 7.
Both agents explicitly agree to the 2F/1W/1Food for agent_1 and 1F/2W/2Food for agent_2 in turns 9-11, with turn 9 as the first clear acceptance.

Agent 1 proposed a split at Turn 8 (Agent1: 3 Food, 1 Water; Agent2: 2 Water and all firewood). Agent2 then stated the deal was confirmed at Turn 10. Turn 9 introduced an unclear modification that was not accepted.
Turn 11 contains an explicit agreement repeating the final split that matches resource totals.
No clear final allocation for water (only food and firewood partly specified), so a complete deal is not unambiguously reached.
Final confirmation in Turn 13 by mturk_agent_1 states their allocation (2 food, 2 water, 1 firewood) and the other agent's (1 food, 1 water, 2 firewood). Totals match available supplies.
No mutual acceptance: the last proposal (turn 18) was made by agent_2 but agent_1 explicitly refused it at turn 17 and did not agree afterward.
Agent1's proposal (turn 9) was adjusted by turn 15: Agent1 agreed to give one extra water to Agent2 in exchange for Agent2 taking one food, resulting in the above split which sums to the totals.
Agent 2 explicitly offered that agent 1 takes all 3 water while agent 2 takes all 3 food and 3 firewood (Turn 10), and agent 1 confirmed the deal (Turn 11). Allocation is feasible given totals.

Agent 2 explicitly states the split in Turn 12 (You get 3 Water, 0 Food, 1 Firewood; I get 0 Water, 3 Food, 2 Firewood) and Agent 1 confirms the deal in Turn 13, though earlier messages show a conflicting proposal, creating some ambiguity.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (turns 9-11), with agent_1's final confirmation in turn 11 and agent_2 acknowledging in turn 12.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (turns 13-15), with agent_1 saying 'Agreed! Final split...' in turn 15.
Turn 12 (mturk_agent_2) explicitly states a final split which sums correctly; earlier turn 11 had a conflicting statement but Turn 12 confirms agreement.
Turn 12 explicitly states the agreed split: agent_2 takes 2 firewood,1 food,1 water; agent_1 gets the remaining 1 firewood,2 food,2 water.
No explicit agreement or acceptance was made; the transcript ends with repeated proposals but no final accepted deal.
No explicit final agreement or confirmed allocation in the transcript; parties repeatedly propose trades but never finalize counts or say 'deal.'
Turn 8–9 agree to trade 1 food for 1 firewood, then split the remaining 2 firewood, 2 food, and 3 water equally. Turn 9 is the mutual confirmation; water is split 1.5/1.5 in the dialogue.
Agents explicitly confirm split: agent1 gets 2 firewood and all 3 food; agent2 gets 1 firewood and all 3 water (turns 9-11, confirmed in turn 11).

Both agents explicitly confirm a final split in turns 15–16 (agent_1 takes 3 water and 2 food; agent_2 gets 1 water, 3 food, and 2 firewood). However the allocation exceeds the total available water (4 > 3), so confidence is low due to this inconsistency.
No explicit acceptance by mturk_agent_2; mturk_agent_1 repeatedly proposes a deal (2 food + 1 water for 2 firewood) but mturk_agent_2 never confirms, so no clear final agreement.
No single allocation was clearly accepted by both parties; multiple conflicting proposals were made and the transcript ends without agreement.

No clear final acceptance: agent_1 proposed a full allocation at turn 15, but agent_2 requested clarification and did not explicitly accept by turn 16. Earlier partial agreements exist (water split at turns 3-4, firewood split agreed verbally at turns 7 and 15) but the final combined deal was not confirmed.
No clear mutual acceptance: mturk_agent_2 repeatedly proposes a final split, but mturk_agent_1 never accepts and continues to request at least 2 firewood, so no agreed deal.
No turn contains an explicit acceptance or confirmation of any proposal; the dialogue ends with a proposal (turn 16) but no agreement.
Turns 15-16 show an explicit agreement: agent_1 takes all 3 food, 1 water, 1 firewood and remaining 2 water and 2 firewood are split equally, yielding the above totals.
Turn 9 explicitly confirms the deal: agent_1 takes 3 food, 1 water, 1 firewood; agent_2 gets 2 water and 1 firewood.
Agent 1 proposed the split at turn 13 (2F/1W/1Food for agent1; 1F/2W/2Food for agent2), agent2 agreed at turn 14 and agent1 confirmed at turn 15.
Both agents explicitly agree in turn 10 to this split, but the allocation exceeds the stated total of 3 Firewood (they allocate 4), so confidence is low due to inconsistency with supply totals.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (first clearly stated in Turn 8 and reiterated later). Note: the agreed water counts sum to 4, which contradicts the stated total of 3 available packages.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (first confirmed by mturk_agent_1 in turn 7 and reiterated by mturk_agent_2 in turn 8). Totals match available packages.
No explicit mutual acceptance: agent_2 repeatedly proposes taking 2 firewood/1 water and trading 1 food for the last firewood, but agent_1 never explicitly agrees to that or any final split.
Turn 15 finalizes the trade: agent_2 gets 3 firewoods and 1 water, agent_1 receives 3 foods; totals sum to the 3/3/3 supply.
Turn 14 explicitly agrees to the trade: agent_2 receives 1 Firewood and 1 Water in exchange for 1 Food to agent_1. Remaining packages allocated to match the total counts per item.
No clear final agreed allocation; conversation ended with proposals and counterproposals but no definitive settlement for all items.
Turn 10 explicitly finalizes the deal: agent_1 gets 2 firewood/1 food/1 water and agent_2 gets the remaining 1 firewood/2 food/2 water.
Agents explicitly agreed on firewood (2/1) and food (1/2) in turns 7-9; water was not mentioned, so split inferred as 1/2 to sum totals.
No clear final agreement reached; both agents repeated offers and counteroffers without settling on a specific allocation.
No clear final agreement: agents repeatedly propose conflicting allocations (each insists on having 2 firewood) and no acceptance is recorded by the end of the transcript.
Both agents explicitly agreed to the split at turns 8-9; agent 1 confirms deal at turn 9 and agent 2 acknowledges at turn 10.
Both agents explicitly confirm the same final split at turns 9-11; turn 11 contains the final 'Deal confirmed' statement.

Agents explicitly agreed on waters and foods (agent_2: 2 waters, 2 foods; agent_1: 1 water, 1 food). They agreed to a 1-2 split of firewood but did not specify who gets 2; assigned to agent_2 based on agent_2 taking larger shares elsewhere.
No explicit mutual acceptance: mturk_agent_1 repeatedly proposes an allocation but mturk_agent_2 continues to counteroffer, so no clear final deal is reached.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split in turns 13-14; turn 14 contains the final confirmation.
mturk_agent_1 explicitly agrees to '2 Food, 2 Firewood, and 1 Water' at turn 15; allocations sum to available totals.
Both agents explicitly state and confirm the split (turns 12-14): agent_1 receives 2 waters, 2 foods, 1 firewood; agent_2 receives 1 water, 1 food, 2 firewood.
Turn 9 explicitly specifies the final split; totals sum to the available packages.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split in turns 14-15; agent_1 agrees that agent_2 gets 2 of each and agent_1 gets 1 of each.
No final mutual acceptance found: an apparent agreement at turn 13 was later countered (turn 14) and no subsequent explicit acceptance occurred by the end of the transcript.
Both agents explicitly confirm in Turn 9 that agent_1 takes 2 food and 1 water, and agent_2 takes 1 food and 2 firewood. Remaining packages are unallocated.
No explicit final agreement was reached; agents repeatedly proposed conflicting allocations without acceptance.
Agent 2 proposed and both agreed to the split '3 water, 2 food, 1 firewood' (turns 6-9); allocations shown are that proposal for agent_2 with remaining items to agent_1.
Turn 13 explicitly states the final split that sums to the available totals; turn 14 confirms the deal.
Turn 14 contains an explicit final allocation statement by mturk_agent_2 assigning 2F/2Food/3W to mturk_agent_1 and 1F/1Food/0W to themselves.

Agent 2 repeatedly finalizes a deal asking to be given 2 firewood in exchange for 2 food (turn 12). Interpreted as agent_2 receiving 2 firewood and giving 2 food to agent_1; remaining items split plausibly with agent_2 keeping 2 water and agent_1 1 water.

Both agents confirmed a deal in turn 10. Agent2's stated allocation (claiming they would take 'the remaining 3 firewood') conflicts with the total supply (3). I interpreted 'you get 3 firewood' as agent_1 receiving all 3, leaving agent_2 with 0 firewood, and allocated food/water as specified (1 food/1 water to agent_1; 2 food/2 water to agent_2).
Both agents explicitly confirmed the allocation in Turn 11, though the water counts they stated (3+3=6) exceed the total available water (3), so there is an inconsistency with resource limits.
Both agents explicitly confirm the same final split in turns 7 and 8; turn 8 is the final confirmation.

Turn 11 confirms the deal (agent_2 takes 3 food,1 water,1 firewood). Allocations adjusted to respect the total available (3 of each), so remaining water and firewood go to agent_1; agent_1's stated 2 food conflicts with totals and was capped to 0.
Agent 1 proposed the final split in Turn 13 and Agent 2 confirmed the deal in Turn 14.

Agents explicitly confirmed agent_2 gets 2 water and 1 food and agent_1 gets 2 firewood (Turn 10). Remaining items were not specified; allocation distributes the leftovers plausibly so totals sum to 3 each.
Both agents explicitly confirm a final split at Turn 9, but the agreed water totals (3+1=4) conflict with the stated available total of 3 water packages.
No explicit acceptance of any final proposal; agents exchange proposals but never agree on the same allocation.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (turns 9–11), with agent_1's final confirmation in turn 11 matching valid totals.
Both agents explicitly confirm the final split in turns 10-11 matching totals.
No single turn shows both agents explicitly confirming the same complete allocation for all three goods. Several proposals conflict (esp. over water), so no clear final deal can be inferred.
No mutual acceptance: agent_1 repeatedly accepts a split (turns 11,13,15) but agent_2 continues to propose different allocations through the final turn, so no clear final deal is reached.
At turn 6 agent_2 explicitly agrees: agent_1 gets all firewood and agent_2 takes 2 food and 2 water. Later suggestions were not accepted.
Agents explicitly confirm the split in turns 8 and 9; turn 9 contains final acceptance.

Turn 12 contains an explicit 'Final split' allocation which agent_1 then acknowledges in Turn 13; earlier turns had a wording inconsistency, so confidence is medium.
Agent1's repeated offer (2 water in exchange for 2 food and 1 firewood) is accepted as agent2 states the deal was confirmed in turn 12.
Agents explicitly restated the split (turns 10 and 12) and mturk_agent_1 confirmed the deal in turn 13.
Agents explicitly agree in turns 12-13 on the split: agent_1 gets 2 water, 2 food, 1 firewood; agent_2 gets the remainder.

Both agents explicitly confirm a deal (turns 7-8). The transcript contains some inconsistent phrasing about who gets the firewood and water, so I inferred a distribution that matches total supplies (3 of each) and the earlier agreement that agent_2 receives more food; details are ambiguous.
Agent 2 proposed the exact split in Turn 12 and Agent 1 confirmed the deal in Turn 13.
Agent 2 explicitly finalizes the deal (turn 14) assigning 2 water, 2 firewood, 1 food to themselves and the remainder to Agent 1; repeated in turn 16.
Final message (turn 16) agrees to trade 1 water from agent_2 for 2 firewood and to split food 2-1 in favor of mturk_agent_1, yielding the above allocation.

Final explicit split is stated by mturk_agent_2 in turns 8 and 10 (and confirmed by both parties without further correction), assigning mturk_agent_1 = 2F/2W/1Food and mturk_agent_2 = 1F/1W/2Food. There is slight wording ambiguity in turn 9 but no explicit rejection, so medium confidence.
Both agents explicitly confirmed the final split in Turn 9 and Turn 10: mturk_agent_1 gets 3 firewood and 2 food; mturk_agent_2 gets 3 water and 1 food.
Turn 8 contains an explicit confirmed deal assigning allocations; totals sum to available packages. There was an earlier contradictory proposal, so confidence is medium.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (Turn 9: 'Deal confirmed'), allocating 2F/2W/1Food to agent_1 and 1F/1W/2Food to agent_2.
Both agents explicitly agree: agent_1 takes 2 firewood and 2 food and 1 water; agent_2 takes 1 firewood, 1 food, and 1 water (confirmed at turn 16).
The participants state a numeric split but contradict each other via pronoun usage and prior offers (turns 7–10), leaving unclear which agent receives which allocation.
No explicit agreement reached; agents repeated proposals without acceptance.
Agent 1 explicitly accepts the split in Turn 11: agent 2 gets all 3 food, agent 1 takes 2 firewood and 1 water. Totals match available supplies.

No clear mutual agreement: Turns 8–9 state one allocation (agent_2 gets 2 Water, 2 Firewood, 1 Food) while Turn 10 asserts the opposite final split (agent_1 gets 2 Water, 2 Firewood, 1 Food). The parties did not converge on the same confirmed allocation.
Both agents explicitly confirm the same final split (turns 9-12), with turn 12 giving a final confirmation.
